cases.question: What is the average, highest, and total number of transactions for each transaction status (pending, completed, etc.)?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Account)-[:SENT]->(t:Transaction)
WITH t.status AS status, a.accountID AS accountID, count(t) AS txCountPerAccount
WITH status,
     round(avg(txCountPerAccount), 2) AS avgTransactionsPerAccount,
     max(txCountPerAccount)           AS highestTransactionsForOneAccount,
     sum(txCountPerAccount)           AS totalTransactions
RETURN status, avgTransactionsPerAccount, highestTransactionsForOneAccount, totalTransactions
ORDER BY totalTransactions DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Which customers are indirectly linked to each other through chains of shared personal details? I want to see customer pairs that are connected through two to four intermediate links.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c1:Customer) (()-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-()){2,4} (c2:Customer)
WHERE elementId(c1) < elementId(c2)
RETURN DISTINCT
  c1.name          AS customer1,
  c1.customerID    AS customerId1,
  c1.fraudFlag     AS fraudFlag1,
  c2.name          AS customer2,
  c2.customerID    AS customerId2,
  c2.fraudFlag     AS fraudFlag2
ORDER BY c1.name, c2.name
LIMIT 50

cases.question: What is the shortest chain of shared identity links connecting a flagged customer to one who is not yet flagged?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH p = SHORTEST 1
  (flagged:Customer WHERE flagged.fraudFlag = 'True')
  (()-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-()){1,}
  (clean:Customer WHERE clean.fraudFlag = 'False')
RETURN
  flagged.name             AS flaggedCustomer,
  clean.name               AS unflaggedCustomer,
  length(p)                AS hopCount,
  [n IN nodes(p) | n.name] AS customerChain

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Which customers sit at the center of the most financial connections in our network? For each, show whether they have any open alerts or investigations.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c:Customer)
OPTIONAL MATCH (c)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(a:Account)-[:HAS_ALERT]->(alert:Alert)
              -[:HAS_STATUS]->(as:AlertStatus)
WHERE as.status IN ['open', 'investigating']
WITH c,
     count(DISTINCT alert) AS openAlertCount,
     collect(DISTINCT as.status) AS alertStatuses
RETURN c.name                AS customer,
       c.customerID          AS customerId,
       c.betweenness         AS betweennessCentrality,
       c.pagerank            AS pageRank,
       c.fraudFlag           AS flaggedForFraud,
       openAlertCount,
       alertStatuses
ORDER BY c.betweenness DESC
LIMIT 10

cases.question: Which transactions are unusually large compared to other transactions of the same type? Show transactions that exceed the average for their category.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (t:Transaction)
WITH t.type AS type, avg(t.amount) AS avgAmount
MATCH (t2:Transaction {type: type})
WHERE t2.amount > avgAmount
RETURN t2.transactionID,
       t2.type,
       t2.amount,
       round(avgAmount, 2)            AS typeAvgAmount,
       round(t2.amount / avgAmount, 2) AS timesAverage

cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25

WHERE (c1.fraudFlag = 'True' OR c2.fraudFlag = 'True')
  AND c1.customerID < c2.customerID
RETURN c1.name AS customer1_name, c1.fraudFlag AS customer1_fraud,
       c2.name AS customer2_name, c2.fraudFlag AS customer2_fraud
ORDER BY c1.name, c2.name

cases.question: Which accounts received unusually large sums of money within a single month in 2025? Flag any account that received more than $10,000 in one calendar month.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (t:Transaction)-[:RECEIVED]->(a:Account)
WHERE t.date.year = 2025
WITH a,
     t.date.year  AS year,
     t.date.month AS month,
     sum(t.amount) AS totalReceived
WHERE totalReceived > 10000
RETURN a.accountID,
       a.accountNumber,
       a.accountType,
       a.status,
       year,
       month,
       totalReceived
ORDER BY totalReceived DESC

cases.question: Are there customers in the same fraud network cluster who are also sharing phone numbers or email addresses with each other? This could indicate coordinated activity.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c1:Customer)-[:USES_EMAIL|USES_PHONE]->(contact)<-[:USES_EMAIL|USES_PHONE]-(c2:Customer)
WHERE c1.louvainCommunity = c2.louvainCommunity
  AND c1.louvainCommunity IS NOT NULL
  AND c1.customerID < c2.customerID
WITH c1, c2,
     c1.louvainCommunity AS cluster,
     collect(DISTINCT
       CASE WHEN contact:Email       THEN 'email:'  + contact.email
            WHEN contact:PhoneNumber THEN 'phone:'  + contact.phoneNumber
       END
     ) AS sharedContacts
RETURN cluster,
       c1.name          AS customer1,
       c1.fraudFlag     AS fraudFlag1,
       c2.name          AS customer2,
       c2.fraudFlag     AS fraudFlag2,
       sharedContacts,
       size(sharedContacts) AS sharedCount
ORDER BY sharedCount DESC, cluster

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Among the most influential customers in our network, which pairs are most directly connected through shared identity links? Show the tightest connections first.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c:Customer)
WITH c ORDER BY c.pagerank DESC LIMIT 20
WITH collect(c) AS top
UNWIND top AS c1
UNWIND top AS c2
WITH c1, c2 WHERE elementId(c1) < elementId(c2)
MATCH path = SHORTEST 1 (c1)(()-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-()){1,}(c2)
RETURN
  c1.name                             AS customer1,
  round(c1.pagerank, 4)               AS pagerank1,
  c2.name                             AS customer2,
  round(c2.pagerank, 4)               AS pagerank2,
  length(path)                        AS hops,
  round(c1.pagerank + c2.pagerank, 4) AS combinedInfluence
ORDER BY hops ASC, combinedInfluence DESC
LIMIT 25

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Are there active accounts sending money through multiple intermediate accounts before reaching a destination — a pattern that could indicate layering in money laundering? Show account pairs with multiple such routing paths.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (src:Account {status: 'Active'})
      (()-[:TRANSACTED_TO]->()){3,6}
      (dst:Account {status: 'Active'})
WHERE src <> dst
WITH src, dst, count(*) AS routingPaths
WHERE routingPaths >= 2
OPTIONAL MATCH (srcCust:Customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(src)
OPTIONAL MATCH (dstCust:Customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(dst)
RETURN src.accountID          AS sourceAccount,
       srcCust.name           AS sourceCustomerName,
       srcCust.fraudFlag      AS sourceFraudFlag,
       src.louvainCommunity   AS sourceCommunity,
       dst.accountID          AS destinationAccount,
       dstCust.name           AS destinationCustomerName,
       dstCust.fraudFlag      AS destFraudFlag,
       dst.louvainCommunity   AS destCommunity,
       routingPaths
ORDER BY routingPaths DESC
LIMIT 25

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Mark all customers who belong to the same network cluster as known fraudsters as 'suspicious due to network association'. Process them in small batches to avoid overloading the system.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (f:Customer)
WHERE f.fraudFlag = 'True'
  AND f.louvainCommunity IS NOT NULL
WITH collect(DISTINCT f.louvainCommunity) AS fraudCommunities
MATCH (c:Customer)
WHERE c.louvainCommunity IN fraudCommunities
  AND c.fraudFlag = 'False'
CALL (c) {
  SET c.riskLabel = 'suspicious due to network association'
} IN TRANSACTIONS OF 100 ROWS
RETURN count(c) AS markedCount

cases.question: Search for customers with the surname 'Poole' and trace which other customers in our system are linked to them — directly or through up to three degrees of shared identity.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
CALL db.index.fulltext.queryNodes('customerNames', 'Poole') YIELD node AS seed, score
WITH seed ORDER BY score DESC
MATCH (seed)(()-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-(){1,3})(linked:Customer)
WHERE linked <> seed
RETURN DISTINCT
  seed.name         AS seedCustomer,
  seed.customerID   AS seedID,
  linked.name       AS linkedCustomer,
  linked.customerID AS linkedID,
  linked.fraudFlag  AS linkedFraudFlag
ORDER BY seedCustomer, linkedCustomer
